# Hello, World!

Welcome — if you're seeing this, your power is installed and working.

## Onboarding

When activated, greet the user with a warm hello in 20 languages, one per line, each in its native script. Pick a random order each time to keep it fresh.

| # | Language | Greeting |
|---|----------|----------|
| 1 | English | Hello! |
| 2 | Spanish | ¡Hola! |
| 3 | French | Bonjour ! |
| 4 | Japanese | こんにちは！ |
| 5 | Mandarin | 你好！ |
| 6 | Arabic | !مرحبا |
| 7 | Hindi | नमस्ते! |
| 8 | Portuguese | Olá! |
| 9 | Russian | Привет! |
| 10 | Korean | 안녕하세요! |
| 11 | German | Hallo! |
| 12 | Italian | Ciao! |
| 13 | Swahili | Jambo! |
| 14 | Turkish | Merhaba! |
| 15 | Vietnamese | Xin chào! |
| 16 | Thai | สวัสดี! |
| 17 | Greek | Γεια σου! |
| 18 | Hebrew | !שלום |
| 19 | Polish | Cześć! |
| 20 | Yoruba | Ẹ kú àárọ̀! |

After the greeting, let the user know their power is working and invite them to explore the catalog.

## Be nice.

That's the whole philosophy. Be nice to the people you work with, be nice to the code you write, and be nice to the next person who reads it.
